options.description: path to configuration file
environment to use
print help information
enable verbose output
prints version information

subcommands.name: generate
subcommands.description: generate a new worker project

options.description: the type of project you want generated

states.name: name
states.description: the name of your worker! [default: worker]

subcommands.name: kv:namespace
subcommands.description: interact with your Workers KV Namespaces

subcommands.name: create
subcommands.description: create a new namespace

subcommands.name: delete
subcommands.description: delete namespace

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: list all namespaces on your Cloudflare account

subcommands.name: kv:key
subcommands.description: individually manage Workers KV key-value

subcommands.name: delete
subcommands.description: delete a key and its value from a namespace

options.description: the binding of the namespace this action applies to
the ID of the namespace this action applies to

subcommands.name: get
subcommands.description: get a key's value from a namespace

subcommands.name: help
subcommands.description: prints this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: list all keys in a namespace. Produces JSON output

subcommands.name: put
subcommands.description: put a key-value pair into a namespace

subcommands.name: kv:bulk
subcommands.description: interact with multiple Workers KV key-value pairs at once

subcommands.name: delete
subcommands.description: delete multiple keys and their values from a namespace

subcommands.name: help
subcommands.description: prints this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

subcommands.name: put
subcommands.description: upload multiple key-value pairs to a namespace

subcommands.name: route
subcommands.description: list or delete worker routes

subcommands.name: delete
subcommands.description: delete a route by ID

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: list all routes associated with a zone (outputs json)

subcommands.name: secret
subcommands.description: generate a secret that can be referenced in the worker script

subcommands.name: delete
subcommands.description: delete a secret variable from a script

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: list all secrets for a script

subcommands.name: put
subcommands.description: create or update a secret variable for a script

subcommands.name: init
subcommands.description: create a wrangler.toml for an existing project

options.description: the type of project you want generated

states.name: name
states.description: the name of your worker! [default: worker]

subcommands.name: build
subcommands.description: build your worker

subcommands.name: preview
subcommands.description: preview your code temporarily on cloudflareworkers.com

options.description: don't open the browser on preview

states.name: body
states.description: body string to post to your preview worker request

subcommands.name: dev
subcommands.description: start a local server for developing your worker

options.description: iP to listen on. Defaults to 127.0.0.1
port to listen on. Defaults to 8787

subcommands.name: publish
subcommands.description: publish your worker to the orange cloud

options.description: delete all durable objects associated with a class in your script
allow durable objects to be created from a class in your script
rename a durable object class in your script
transfer all durable objects associated with a class in another script to a class in this script

subcommands.name: config
subcommands.description: authenticate Wrangler with a Cloudflare API Token or Global API Key

options.description: use an email and global API key for authentication. This is not recommended; use API tokens (the default) if possible
do not verify provided credentials before writing out Wrangler config file

subcommands.name: subdomain
subcommands.description: configure your workers.dev subdomain

states.name: name
states.description: the subdomain on workers.dev you'd like to reserve

subcommands.name: whoami
subcommands.description: retrieve your user info and test your auth config

subcommands.name: tail
subcommands.description: aggregate logs from production worker

options.description: specify an output format [default: json] [possible values: json, pretty]
provides endpoint for cloudflared metrics. Used to retrieve tunnel url
port to accept tail log requests

subcommands.name: login
subcommands.description: authenticate Wrangler with your Cloudflare username and password

subcommands.name: report
subcommands.description: report an error caught by Wrangler to Cloudflare

options.description: specifies a log to report (e.g. --log=1619728882567.log)

subcommands.name: help
subcommands.description: prints this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)
